acceptance.assertion: Mechanic and Captain sequence logic lives outside the core grip surface file.
Generic run_grip dispatch remains runner-agnostic and has no runner-name special cases.
The refactor does not add merge, deploy, service restart, fleet mutation, cleanup or secret authority.

goal: Move Mechanic and Captain sequence/orchestration logic out of grabowski_grips.py into a dedicated orchestration module without changing action authority semantics.

metadata.reason: Keep boundary and Captain authority slices focused before structural extraction.

validated_acceptance.engine-boundary: The generic _RUNNERS dispatch remains runner-key based and runner-name agnostic; the new boundary test asserts wrapper code contains no action loop bodies.
validated_acceptance.no-authority-change: The patch only moves existing orchestration bodies and adds the new module to runtime supporting-source metadata; external review PASS and full tests did not identify authority expansion.
validated_acceptance.orchestrator-module: src/grabowski_grip_orchestration.py now contains run_mechanic_loop, run_captain_preflight and run_captain_run. src/grabowski_grips.py retains thin wrappers only.

validation.ci: GitHub validate (3.10) and validate (3.12) completed SUCCESS for PR #125.

non_claims: Does not add merge, deploy, service restart, fleet mutation, cleanup or secret authority.

Does not prove semantic correctness beyond tests, review and live runtime identity.
